notes: kiro-cli has NEITHER a native autonomous self-prompting loop NOR a native per-turn iteration cap in chat (max_iterations is a multi-agent LoopConfig/AgentCrew/PipelineStage field, not a chat per-turn cycle cap; the turn-end reason enum UserTurnEnd|ToolUseRejected|Cancelled|Error|Rewind has no cap-hit variant). The loop is INTERNAL to one user turn: one seeded explicitly-bounded multi-step prompt ('do exactly 2 steps then STOP') drives the agent's OWN sequential tool cycles (AM(toolUse) -> ToolResults -> ... -> AM(text-only)) with no user prompts between iterations. Because every mid-turn AssistantMessage carries toolUse blocks (parsed 'assistant', keeps working) and every ToolResults is 'tool_result' (keeps working), the daemon sees ONE continuous working span across all internal iterations, and the cap-hit lands as a SINGLE terminal working->ready at the final text-only AssistantMessage (turn_done). The DISTINGUISHING assertion vs the autonomous-loop sibling is the TERMINAL IDLE DWELL: after that terminal working->ready, the session must settle to ready and DWELL there with an invariant of NO further state_transition to working — proving the loop stopped cleanly at its self-imposed limit and did not stick in working or spuriously re-prompt. No waiting under --trust-all-tools (tools auto-approved). kiro-cli's JSONL has no model/token/cost (sidecar-only), so observations assert agent identity but NOT model/cost/tokens.

text: Session appears in ready (kiro mints its UUID and writes the .jsonl lazily after launch). Anchored UNPINNED to start so a transient proc-PID presession row can't steal the birth.
Spec: 'ready -> working at loop start' — the one seeded explicitly-bounded multi-step prompt drives the agent into its autonomous internal loop

Spec: 'agent reaches its configured max-iteration count, emits a terminal turn signal and stops self-prompting; working -> ready'. The session holds a single continuous working span across >= 2 internal tool cycles (assistant + tool_result events) and flips to ready exactly once, at the final text-only AssistantMessage (the agent's 'done' reply) — the cap-hit terminal transition. No waiting arises (--trust-all-tools auto-approves; no permission gates).

Spec: 'state reaches ready even though the agent didn't fully succeed; does not stick in working indefinitely'. Iteration limit reached: the loop stops. The session DWELLS in ready for at least 7s of trailing idle with NO further working transition (no stuck-working, no spurious post-limit re-prompt) and never any waiting. Final state: ready.
